subject: Project Management
title: The NHS IT Disaster
description: A quick win: understand The NHS IT Disaster.

hook.text: In 2002, the UK launched a £2.3 billion project to digitize the National Health Service. It cost £12 billion.

buildup.text: The National Programme for IT would connect every hospital, GP, and pharmacy in England.

discovery.text: Contractors delivered systems that doctors refused to use — too slow, too complex, and poorly designed.

twist.text: Major contractors like Accenture and Fujitsu walked away from the project rather than continue.

climax.text: In 2011, the government officially scrapped it — the UK's most expensive IT failure ever.

punchline.text: Twelve billion pounds. Zero working system.

quiz.question: What happened to the UK's NHS IT programme?
options: It was scrapped after costing £12 billion with no working system
It was sold to a private company
